content.method: Administrative data mapping of maintainer-type enrollment shares over time; legal analysis of church/private autonomy protections and precedent (e.g., Tiszavasvári case) via case-law review; scenario modeling of demand displacement elasticities using historical episodes of state-sector capacity changes.
content.question: What proportion of six/eight-year gimnázium capacity is controlled by non-state maintainers (church, foundation, private, nationality-government), and how would church/private schools respond to state-sector capacity contraction (T2) or cross-maintainer gatekeeping (T6)?
content.why_it_matters: This is decisive for whether T2's state-sector-only lever is undermined by substitution, and whether T6's legal extension to church/private admissions is feasible or will trigger successful constitutional/autonomy challenges—directly affecting the net segregation-reduction achievable by administrative (non-legislative) tools.
